compositional_truth_check.details: The registry indicates P1-P4. Visual evidence confirms only two distinct pot structures: one yellow (P1) and one black (P3/P4). P2 is absent or merged into the black pot structure.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Present (Yellow Pot). Appears as bare soil/substrate.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss. No visible foliage corresponding to this species.
inventory_reconciliation.P4_Silver_Guest: Present (Black Pot). Likely the small sprout visible in the black pot.
new_introductions: White cylindrical object (likely a sensor or probe) inserted into P1.

plant_audit.P1: Bare soil, no visible plant matter. Sensor probe present.
plant_audit.P3: Minimal vegetative presence. Soil surface contains white debris (possibly perlite or eggshell fragments).
plant_audit.P4: Single small green sprout visible in the black pot.

biome_observations.debris: White granular fragments (eggshell/perlite) present in P3/P4. No fungal blooms detected.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: The presence of a sensor probe in P1 and a spoon in T-3 suggests active human intervention/experimentation.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_T_minus_2: Static state. No significant growth or decay observed.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_Current: Minimal change. The sprout in P4 remains in a state of stasis.

visual_health_inference.P3: Stressed. Lack of foliage suggests either extreme pruning or failure to thrive.
visual_health_inference.P4: Stable but fragile. Single sprout indicates minimal biological activity.

anomalies: White granular debris on soil surface.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of severe depletion. The expected botanical specimens are largely absent or reduced to a single, non-thriving sprout. The presence of non-botanical items (sensor, spoon) suggests the environment is being used for testing or monitoring rather than active cultivation. The lack of light and potential over-saturation of the soil may be contributing to the systemic loss of the registered plants.
